target.title: fix(logger): guard call-log writes after close so late events cannot crash the server
target.body:
## Summary

**Classification: runtime bug fix — single-call defect with whole-server blast radius (process crash).**

`createCallLogger`'s WriteStream has no closed-state guard and no `error` listener. Any `logger.event(...)` after `logger.close()` writes to an ended stream, which emits `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END` on a stream with **no error listener** — an uncaught exception that kills the entire `vox serve` process and every concurrent call on it.

This is reachable in production via an async path that #5's `closed` guard in `server.ts` doesn't cover:

1. The model calls `query_agent`; `handleResponseDone` is fired-and-forgotten with a `.catch` that logs `tool.error` (`src/server.ts:235-247`).
2. The caller hangs up while the agent query is in flight → Twilio `stop` / WS close → `cleanupCall()` closes the agent client **and the logger**.
3. The pending agent query rejects (`"Agent process closed"`) → the `.catch` calls `logger.event("vox", { type: "tool.error", ... })` → write-after-end → uncaught exception → server down.

A hung agent + impatient caller is the *normal* failure mode this path exists for, so the crash triggers exactly when the system is already degraded.

### The fix

All inside `src/logger.ts` (14 lines), so every call-site — including future ones — is covered at the single chokepoint:

- `event()` becomes a no-op once the logger is closed. Late events are dropped deliberately: the call is over and `events.jsonl` is complete; previously those events were never persisted either (the process died instead).
- `close()` is idempotent.
- The stream gets an `error` listener that warns on stderr, so even a genuine fs error (ENOSPC, EBADF) degrades to a lost log line instead of a dead bridge.

No behavior change for any live call path; nothing about audio, barge-in, or tool dispatch is touched.

## Reproducibility — deterministic, from production code

`test/server-teardown.test.ts` drives the **real `handleTwilioSocket`** with a **real subprocess agent** (`node -e "process.stdin.resume()"` — accepts the query, never answers) through the exact production sequence above. Unlike a timing-dependent live-call defect, the crash mechanism here is the local fs WriteStream — the process death is real and reproduces deterministically. Only the two WS transports are stubbed; the defect does not involve them.

Against the unfixed logger (`git checkout origin/main -- src/logger.ts`):

```
$ node --import tsx --test test/server-teardown.test.ts
not ok 1 - hang-up during an in-flight agent query does not crash the process
  failureType: 'uncaughtException'
  error: 'write after end'
  code: 'ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END'
# tests 1
# pass 0
# fail 1
```

That `uncaughtException` is the real process-killer observed end-to-end, not an assertion on mocked state. With the fix restored, the same test passes and asserts `events.jsonl` ends cleanly (contains `twilio.ws.closed`, does **not** contain a post-teardown `tool.error` line).

`test/logger.test.ts` additionally pins the unit-level contract: events before close are persisted, events after close are dropped, `close()` is idempotent. The post-close test also fails on the unfixed logger (verified the same way: 3 tests → pass 2 / fail 1, uncaught `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`).

## Test plan — local run output (proof)

Ran the exact CI pipeline (`lint` → `typecheck` → `test` → `build`) on both matrix versions.

### Node v22.22.3

```
$ pnpm run lint
> oxfmt --check .
All matched files use the correct format.
> oxlint --deny-warnings src test
(clean exit)

$ pnpm run typecheck
> tsgo -p tsconfig.json --noEmit
(clean exit, no output)

$ pnpm test
# tests 32
# pass 32
# fail 0
# cancelled 0
# skipped 0
# todo 0
# duration_ms 539.129

$ pnpm run build
ESM dist/cli.js     33.53 KB
ESM dist/cli.js.map 66.25 KB
ESM ⚡️ Build success in 35ms
```

### Node v24.16.0

```
$ pnpm test
ℹ tests 32
ℹ pass 32
ℹ fail 0
ℹ cancelled 0
ℹ skipped 0
ℹ todo 0
ℹ duration_ms 487.813792
```

## Contributor baseline workflow run

The CI workflow also triggers on `push` (`branches: ["**"]`), so the exact head commit of this PR already ran the full pipeline end-to-end on GitHub's hosted infrastructure:

**Run:** https://github.com/joeVenner/vox/actions/runs/27379247773 (original contributor commit `d58f02fa6e10423029aca098e6efeb9f448a582e`) — both matrix jobs succeeded: `test (22)` and `test (24)`.

Live output from the hosted run confirming the new tests executed there:

```
test (22)  Run pnpm test   # tests 32
test (22)  Run pnpm test   # pass 32
test (22)  Run pnpm test   # fail 0
test (22)  Run pnpm run build  ESM ⚡️ Build success in 24ms
test (24)  Run pnpm test   ℹ tests 32
test (24)  Run pnpm test   ℹ pass 32
test (24)  Run pnpm test   ℹ fail 0
test (24)  Run pnpm run build  ESM ⚡️ Build success in 29ms
```

## Risk notes

- The only intentional behavior change is that events arriving after teardown are dropped instead of (attempted to be) written. Before this PR they were never written anyway — the write crashed the server. If post-teardown events should be persisted instead, that requires keeping the stream open past `cleanupCall()`, which is a different lifecycle decision; happy to follow up if preferred.
- Security: no dependency, workflow, permission, or secret-handling changes.


## Maintainer update — final candidate `47f41a90a754`

I independently reproduced the current-`main` failure in a child process using the real `handleTwilioSocket` and subprocess agent path: the process exited with `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END` after hang-up rejected the in-flight query.

The contributor logger guard is retained, but the final candidate also repairs the broader teardown race instead of merely hiding it:

- `createCallLogger` drops post-close writes, closes idempotently, handles stream errors, and stops accepting writes after an underlying stream failure.
- `handleResponseDone` receives call-lifecycle state, stops after teardown, does not resume a closed OpenAI session, and suppresses the expected agent-close rejection instead of attempting a late `tool.error` log.
- Focused regressions cover late events after teardown, logger double-close, overlapping Twilio `stop` and duplicate WebSocket close callbacks, single transport cleanup, and no late OpenAI function output.
- `CHANGELOG.md` records the user-facing crash fix and credits @joeVenner.

### Final verification

- Focused: `node --import tsx --test test/logger.test.ts test/server-teardown.test.ts test/server-truncate.test.ts` — 10 passed.
- Full CI-equivalent gate: frozen install, lint, typecheck, 33 tests, and build all passed.
- Structured review: `$autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests 'pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm run lint && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm run build'` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- PR CI on exact head: Node.js 22, Node.js 24, and GitGuardian all passed: https://github.com/steipete/vox/actions/runs/27380834533
- Real built-server proof on exact pushed SHA: `dist/cli.js` handled a safe owned-number Twilio/OpenAI call where OpenAI issued one `query_agent` function call and the subprocess agent deliberately remained in flight through hang-up. The call completed; Twilio teardown was logged; the Vox process and local/public health endpoints remained alive at HTTP 200; `events.jsonl` ended cleanly; no function output resumed the closed session; and tool, OpenAI, logger/write-after-end error counts were all zero. The temporary destination webhook was restored successfully.

The ClawSweeper comments above are transport-failure states, not findings against the patch; maintainer autoreview completed successfully on the final branch.
